commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=1044ce87e1d3ebb81c79d2c5f82928e4ab701f77)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a default-off Control UI Recent Sessions sidebar All agents toggle, unscopes sidebar refreshes when enabled, allows spawned/subagent rows in that sidebar mode, and adds focused UI/session tests plus checkpoint refresh scoping.

PR surface: Source +85, Tests +183. Total +268 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main filters subagent and spawnedBy rows out of Recent Sessions, while the PR head shows how the new all-agents refresh writes unscoped rows into shared session state.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can isolate the sidebar All agents result from Sessions-table state; maintainer selection among sibling fixes still gates final merge.

target.title: fix #95295: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/95295
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: The Web UI sidebar Recent Sessions list could hide existing child-spawned subagent transcripts when the current chat scope was another agent, so users could not intentionally rediscover those sessions from the left sidebar.
- Solution: Keep the existing scoped/default behavior, but add an explicit sidebar **All agents** mode for sidebar-owned refreshes only. Checkpoint and Sessions-tab mutation refreshes stay scoped to the session's agent.
- What changed: Added sidebar state and UI for the scope toggle, made sidebar-owned requests omit scoped `agentId`, kept cron/global/unknown/archived rows excluded, and added a follow-up checkpoint regression so All agents cannot leak into checkpoint branch/restore refreshes.
- What did NOT change / out of scope: This does not change the chat picker, default sidebar scoping, shared `loadSessions` explicit `agentId` semantics, gateway protocol schema, stored transcript format, migration behavior, public config, or default visibility of subagent/spawned sessions. Making All agents a global Sessions-tab mode is intentionally out of scope.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users with persisted child-spawned subagent work can intentionally find and reopen those existing transcripts from the sidebar without losing the safer scoped default view, and checkpoint/Sessions-tab actions no longer inherit that wider sidebar-only visibility.
- Fixes #95295

## What Problem This Solves

The sidebar needed an intentional way to rediscover persisted child-spawned subagent sessions across agent scopes, but the first pass made the sidebar All agents state too easy to reuse outside the sidebar boundary. In particular, checkpoint branch/restore actions from the Sessions table could refresh `sessions.list` without an agent scope after All agents was enabled. This update keeps the opt-in sidebar behavior while deriving checkpoint refresh scope from the checkpoint session key itself, so Sessions-tab/checkpoint refreshes remain scoped.

## Evidence

- Focused checkpoint regression, requested UI test set, oxlint, and whitespace checks all passed locally after the follow-up checkpoint-scope repair.
- Existing UI/session-store proof from the prior revision still applies for the sidebar All agents behavior: scoped main results omit the orchestrator child, unscoped All agents results include the child-spawned row with canonical `spawnedBy`, and Chrome showed the subagent row only after pressing **All agents**.
- Proof gap: I still cannot run a long-lived live gateway/Mantis proof in this contributor environment. The local proof uses focused repository tests plus the existing deterministic Control UI/session-store browser proof; it does not claim real Mantis or private live-gateway coverage.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Sidebar Recent Sessions can opt in to cross-agent discovery so an existing row such as `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` with `spawnedBy` can appear when the user enables **All agents**, while default scoped mode still hides those rows. The follow-up repair also addresses the review-found scope leak by keeping checkpoint branch/restore `sessions.list` refreshes scoped to the checkpoint session's agent even when sidebar All agents is enabled.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw repository worktree on Windows/Git Bash with Node `v22.17.0`, pnpm-installed workspace dependencies, and installed Chrome driven by Playwright for the earlier deterministic Control UI proof. The repo engine warning requested Node `>=22.19.0`, but the focused Vitest/lint/diff checks and earlier browser/session-store proof completed successfully.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts -t "keeps checkpoint branch and restore refreshes scoped"`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`; `git diff --check`. Earlier proof also ran the Control UI browser flow and a persisted-store `sessions.list` gateway-handler proof for the sidebar All agents behavior.
- **Evidence after fix:** The focused checkpoint regression exited 0 and asserted branch/restore for `agent:review:chat` refresh `sessions.list` with `agentId: "review"` even when `sidebarRecentSessionsAllAgents` is true. The requested four-file UI command exited 0 and includes the new checkpoint-scope regression plus the sidebar renderer/helper coverage. Oxlint on the changed session controller files exited 0. `git diff --check` exited 0. The earlier stronger session-store/browser proof showed scoped main results omit the orchestrator child, unscoped all-agents results include the child row with canonical `spawnedBy`, and Chrome shows `Subagent: Planner` only after **All agents** is pressed.
- **Observed result after fix:** Sidebar-owned All agents refreshes remain unscoped only for the sidebar Recent Sessions list, while checkpoint branch/restore refreshes derive their list scope from the checkpoint session key (`agent:review:*` -> `agentId: "review"`) or from the selected global agent for `global`. This preserves the default scoped view and prevents the sidebar toggle from widening Sessions-tab/checkpoint refreshes.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a long-lived live gateway or real Mantis/private transcript proof in this environment. I also did not expand testing into unrelated protocol/build/typecheck matrices beyond the requested focused UI command, the new checkpoint regression, oxlint on changed session controller files, and `git diff --check`.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 (`status: waiting on author` label):** Addressed by this update; the label can clear after bot/maintainer re-review.
- **RF-002 (focused regression for All agents enabled plus opening checkpoints):** Fixed. Added `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts` coverage for checkpoint branch and restore while `sidebarRecentSessionsAllAgents` is true.
- **RF-003 (P1 checkpoint/Sessions-tab scope leak):** Fixed. Checkpoint branch/restore refresh now calls a session-key-derived list-scope helper, so `agent:review:*` checkpoint actions refresh with `agentId: "review"` instead of using an unscoped/sidebar-wide list request.
- **RF-004 (sibling candidate PRs / canonical product choice):** Disclosed. PRs #95304 and #95307 remain sibling candidates; this PR intentionally keeps the repair UI-only and same-contract. Final product/canonical selection remains a maintainer decision.
- **RF-005 (narrow automated repair):** Fixed with the narrow repair ClawSweeper suggested: sidebar All agents behavior remains, but checkpoint/Sessions-tab refreshes stay scoped.
- **RF-006 (keep checkpoint loads out of sidebar toggle):** Fixed. Checkpoint refresh scope no longer reads or inherits the sidebar toggle; it derives from the checkpoint session key or selected global agent.
- **RF-007 (requested focused Vitest command):** Completed locally with exit 0: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`.
- **RF-008 (`git diff --check`):** Completed locally with exit 0.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`, with neighboring focused suite `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` included in the requested command.
- **Target test files:** `ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts`, `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`, and `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts`, with `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts` included in the requested command.
- **Scenario locked in:** Explicit non-sidebar `loadSessions(..., { agentId })` requests keep their scoped `agentId`; the sidebar All agents control is reachable with an empty recent list; a cross-agent `agent:orchestrator:subagent:*` row with `spawnedBy` renders in Recent Sessions all-agents mode while cron rows remain filtered; and checkpoint branch/restore refreshes stay scoped to the checkpoint session's agent even when the sidebar All agents toggle is active.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** The controller tests guard the request boundary so checkpoint/Sessions-tab refreshes cannot be accidentally widened, the helper/render tests guard sidebar-owned All agents behavior, and no gateway/protocol/storage contract is expanded.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** session-state / security-boundary / compatibility.
- **Risk explanation:** The diff touches session-state UI filtering and a scope control, which can affect whether internal child-spawned transcripts are visible. Compatibility risk is intentionally limited because explicit `loadSessions` agent overrides remain authoritative and no public protocol, schema, config, storage format, or migration contract changes. Related sibling PRs #95304 and #95307 remain broader/candidate approaches; this PR deliberately stays on a narrower same-contract UI path.
- **Why acceptable:** The new behavior is default-off, explicitly user-selected, and limited to the sidebar Recent Sessions list. Existing exclusions for archived/global/unknown/cron rows remain. Checkpoint and Sessions-tab refreshes now stay scoped, so the All agents state does not widen non-sidebar refreshes.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The original sidebar bug was a request/filter invariant mismatch: Recent Sessions requested current-agent rows and then filtered out subagent/spawned rows, making persisted child-spawned sessions from another agent impossible to rediscover from the sidebar. The follow-up review found a second boundary bug: the sidebar All agents request helper was reused by refreshes that are not sidebar-owned.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** Sidebar-owned refreshes now own the All agents opt-in behavior, while checkpoint refreshes derive their agent scope from the session key. That keeps the sidebar request/filter invariant correct without changing shared gateway contracts or making All agents a global Sessions-tab mode.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix with a bounded follow-up repair for the review-found scope leak.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the scoped code/test repair; maintainer product selection remains required because sibling PRs exist with different scopes.
- **Patch quality notes:** The `state as unknown as Parameters<typeof loadSessions>[0]` cast remains confined to the existing UI helper boundary where `AppViewState` is structurally passed to `loadSessions`; the follow-up checkpoint repair does not add another type escape. The added `return false` branches in `app-render.ts` are explicit filter-preserving guard branches for cron/global/unknown/archived/default-off subagent rows, not symptom-masking fallbacks. The public-contract warning is addressed by keeping the canonical protocol/schema unchanged and using existing unscoped `sessions.list` only from the explicit sidebar toggle request. The diff is larger than an XS patch because it includes the original UI state/control/render coverage plus this focused checkpoint-scope repair; the review blocker itself is fixed in the small controller/test follow-up.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The source-of-truth boundary remains the existing `sessions.list` contract and combined session store. Sidebar Recent Sessions owns the **All agents** opt-in request/filter behavior; checkpoint/Sessions-tab mutation refreshes own their selected-session scope and derive `agentId` from the session key. This patch does not add schema, protocol, public config, data-format, storage, migration, or gateway-contract changes.
target.author: mikasa0818

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
